steps.title: Check equal-angle cases
steps.content: Triangle $ABC$ is isosceles, so two angles must be equal. Since angle $A$ is $40^{\circ}$, there are three cases: $A=B$, $A=C$, or $B=C$.

steps.title: List the possible values
steps.content: If $A=B$, then $B=40^{\circ}$. If $A=C$, then $$B=180^{\circ}-40^{\circ}-40^{\circ}=100^{\circ}$$ If $B=C$, then $$40^{\circ}+2B=180^{\circ}$$ $$B=70^{\circ}$$ So $110^{\circ}$ is not possible. The answer is D.
